content.full_text:
February 23, 1989




Bob Connelly
Chief Administrative Officer
Assembly Rules Committee
State Capitol, Room 3016
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: Letter No. 89-113

Dear Mr. Connelly:

You have asked for confirmation of advice given concerning
the press release exemption in Regulation 18901. Your letter
accurately states the advice we have given.

Sincerely,

[illegible]

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

MEMBERS
BILL FILANTE,
Vice Chair
JACK O'CONNELL
Majority Vice Chair

CHIEF ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICER
BOB CONNELLY

LUCY KILLEA
RICHARD MOUNTJOY
PAT NOLAN
RICHARD POLANCO
JACKIE SPEIER
CATHIE WRIGHT

EXECUTIVE SECRETARY
TERRIE F. WILFONG

California Legislature
Assembly Rules Committee
ROOM 3016 — STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 94249-0001
TELEPHONE (916) 445-8173

TOM BANE
CHAIRMAN

February 9, 1989

Ms. Diane Griffith
General Counsel
F.P.P.C.
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. Griffith:

I am writing to confirm telephone advice received by Barbara
Milman from John McLean on January 12, 1989, and from yourself
on February 7, 1989, concerning the exception for press
releases in regulation 18901.

You and Mr. McLean advised:

1.    Any communication sent to the media is a press
      release, whether or not it is titled "Press
      Release".

2.    Press releases sent to high school newspapers are
      press releases within the meaning of the
      regulation.

I would appreciate receiving written confirmation of this
advice.

Sincerely,

BOB CONNELLY
Chief Administrative Officer

BC:BM:dd

sections.question_synthetic: What constitutes a press release under Regulation 18901, specifically whether communications sent to media (including high school newspapers) qualify as press releases under the exemption?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms prior telephone advice that any communication sent to the media is a press release regardless of title, and that press releases sent to high school newspapers qualify as press releases under Regulation 18901.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (dated February 9, 1989) and FPPC response (dated February 23, 1989). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; question and conclusion derived from letter content. Confirmation letter format rather than detailed advisory response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What constitutes a press release under Regulation 18901, specifically whether communications sent to media (including high school newspapers) qualify as press releases under the exemption?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC confirms prior telephone advice that any communication sent to the media is a press release regardless of title, and that press releases sent to high school newspapers qualify as press releases under Regulation 18901.
